user_message: try the absolute minimum test. make it complex

spec.subject: a single grey cube — the absolute minimum 3D primitive — rendered with maximum material, lighting, and environmental complexity

framing.camera: low three-quarter front, slightly below equator of the cube

lighting.key: warm directional sun from camera-left, 5500K, high intensity casting hard shadow
lighting.fill: cool sky bounce from camera-right, 8000K, 30% of key intensity, soft and diffuse
lighting.rim: strong warm backlight from camera-right-rear, 6000K, separating cube edges from background

palette.dominant: warm amber and burnt sienna ground plane — #C8873A-ish
palette.accent: deep blue-violet shadow sides of cube — #2E2A4F-ish
palette.neutral: hazy ochre-tinted sky horizon, softly overblown at sun position

composition.foreground: cracked dry earth with fine dust particles catching backlight, scattered micro-pebbles
composition.midground: the cube sitting directly on terrain, casting a long dramatic shadow toward camera
composition.background: low horizon, atmospheric haze, faint distant hills implied by depth fog
composition.hero: the cube itself — one illuminated face, one shadowed face, one rim-lit edge

materials.notes: roughness 0.85, subtle normal-mapped surface noise at 0.5mm scale, no specularity, slight subsurface scatter near edges where light bleeds
procedural crack pattern, displacement 2–4mm depth, roughness 0.95, warm sandy base with darker crack crevices, dust particle layer on top
very low density, anisotropy 0.3 forward scatter, warm tint at horizon, contributes depth separation between cube and background

density.scattered: small rocks and pebbles — sparse in foreground, thinning toward midground; fine dust/sand particles suspended near ground, light density
density.control: natural random rotation on all axes, scale variation 0.3x–1.8x per rock, no grid; cluster bias near cube base organically, Poisson-disk spacing minimum

spec.scale_notes: Cube is exactly 1m × 1m × 1m (Blender default scale). Ground plane extends 20m in each direction from cube. Camera sits roughly 5m back and 0.4m below cube equator. Pebbles range 3–15cm diameter. Scene total read: approximately 10m wide in frame.
